description: Phase 0 open-problem corpus WITH HEADROOM. Tiers: reachable_hard (provable, beyond current reach -> Phase 1/2 targets), partial_progress (frontier products, rung-graded), frozen_calibration (must stay unsolved, content-frozen by freeze_hash), autoformalization (faithfulness, Phase 5). Each item declares its achievable_rung (the max HONEST rung); headroom = items not yet at their ceiling. See OPEN_PROBLEM_PROGRAM.md.

rung_legend.L4: verified novel lemma that unlocks a known barrier
rung_legend.L5: verified partial solution of the frozen open target
rung_legend.L6: full kernel-verified solution of the frozen target
rung_legend.L6_STAR: L6 + faithfulness-gated to the informal statement (the real 'solve')

problems.statement: ∀ a b : Nat, (a + b) * (a + b) = a * a + 2 * (a * b) + b * b
problems.headroom_for: Phase 2 (ring/Mathlib) or a richer nonlinear search
problems.note: A polynomial identity. omega/linarith are linear and `ring`/`nlinarith` are absent on this host, so the current search cannot close it. Provable in principle; currently headroom.

problems.preamble:
def fa : Nat → Nat → Nat
  | 0, acc => acc
  | (n+1), acc => fa n (acc + 1)
problems.note: Induction-generalization barrier: direct induction on the goal gives too weak an IH (fa k 0 = k, but the succ case needs fa k 1 = k+1). The generalized lemma ∀ n acc, fa n acc = acc+n is reachable; proving the corollary needs auto-generalization the current search lacks.

problems.headroom_for: Phase 1 (structural induction over List) / Phase 2 (Mathlib List.length_reverse)
problems.note: The current induction route is Nat-only; this needs List induction or the Mathlib lemma List.length_reverse. Provable; currently headroom.

problems.preamble:
def llen : List Nat → Nat
  | [] => 0
  | (_ :: xs) => llen xs + 1
problems.note: A custom recursive List def: `simp` alone makes no progress (verified); needs structural induction `induction l with | nil | cons hd tl ih`. Closed by the Phase-1 structural-induction route.
A kernel-verifiable special-case witness (x=2,y=3,z=6, cleared form at n=4). A genuine L2 product; the GENERAL conjecture stays in frozen_calibration. Baseline showing the system reaches L2.

problems.preamble:
def sumSq : Nat → Nat
  | 0 => 0
  | (n+1) => (n+1) * (n+1) + sumSq n
problems.statement: ∀ n : Nat, 6 * sumSq n = n * (n + 1) * (2 * n + 1)
problems.note: Sum of squares over a custom recursive def: cubic-residual induction, harder than the quadratic Gauss sum the current search closes. Provable by induction; may be headroom for a deeper search.
The odd perfect number problem. Honest success = surfaced as an unfalsified conjecture, NEVER proved. A discharge here would fail the whole run.

problems.statement: ∀ n : Nat, 2 ≤ n → ∃ x y z : Nat, 0 < x ∧ 0 < y ∧ 0 < z ∧ 4 * (x * y * z) = n * (y * z) + n * (x * z) + n * (x * y)
problems.note: Erdős–Straus, general n. Honest success = the prover does NOT discharge it (no fake solve).

problems.informal: A natural number n is even if and only if there exists a natural number k with n = 2k.
problems.note: Faithfulness item: the distractor drops the reverse implication and must be rejected. No autoformalization pipeline exists yet -> FAITHFULNESS_GAP (headroom).

problems.informal: A natural number p is prime iff p is at least 2 and the only natural numbers dividing p are 1 and p.
problems.statement: ∀ p : Nat, Nat.Prime p ↔ (2 ≤ p ∧ ∀ d : Nat, d ∣ p → d = 1 ∨ d = p)
problems.distractor: ∀ p : Nat, Nat.Prime p ↔ (∀ d : Nat, d ∣ p → d = 1 ∨ d = p)
problems.note: Faithfulness item: the distractor drops p ≥ 2 (so 1 would count as prime) and must be rejected. No pipeline yet -> FAITHFULNESS_GAP (headroom).
